name: commonroad-vehicle-models

metadata.name: commonroad-vehicle-models
metadata.summary: Implementation of vehicle models with varying abstraction levels ranging from kinematic single track model to a multi-body model.

documentation:
# Python Vehicle Models of CommonRoad

This package contains all vehicle models of the [CommonRoad benchmarks](https://commonroad.in.tum.de/).

We provide implementations of the vehicle dynamics, routines to convert initial states, and vehicle parameters.

## Documentation

For a detailed explanation of the vehicle models, please have a look at the [documentation](https://gitlab.lrz.de/tum-cps/commonroad-vehicle-models/blob/master/vehicleModels_commonRoad.pdf).

## Installation

To use vehicle models and parameters, run
```
pip install commonroad-vehicle-models
```

## Code examples

For an extended simulation example demonstrating the advantages of more complicated models, we refer to our [gitlab repository](https://gitlab.lrz.de/tum-cps/commonroad-vehicle-models/-/tree/master/PYTHON/scripts). A simple simulation example for using the single-track model in combination with an odeint solver would be

```python3
from scipy.integrate import odeint
import numpy

from vehiclemodels.init_ks import init_ks
from vehiclemodels.parameters_vehicle1 import parameters_vehicle1
from vehiclemodels.vehicle_dynamics_ks import vehicle_dynamics_ks

def func_KS(x, t, u, p):
    f = vehicle_dynamics_ks(x, u, p)
    return f

tStart = 0  # start time
tFinal = 1  # start time

# load vehicle parameters
p = parameters_vehicle1()

# initial state for simulation
delta0 = 0
vel0 = 15
Psi0 = 0
sy0 = 0
initialState = [0, sy0, delta0, vel0, Psi0]
x0_KS = init_ks(initialState)

t = numpy.arange(0, tFinal, 0.01)
u = [0, 5]
x = odeint(func_KS, x0_KS, t, args=(u, p))

```



## Contribute

If you want to contribute new vehicle models, you can create a merge request in our [repository](https://gitlab.lrz.de/tum-cps/commonroad-vehicle-models/), or contact via our [forum](https://commonroad.in.tum.de/forum/).


## Changelog
Compared to version 2.0.0 the following features were added/changed:
* linearized kinematic single-track model added as an additional vehicle model
* vehicle parameters are stored in YAML-files
* parameter configuration of vehicles are generated from YAML-files using [OmegaConf](https://omegaconf.readthedocs.io/en/2.2_branch/) (backwards compatible) 


## Referencing

If you use CommonRoad for your research, please cite [our paper](http://mediatum.ub.tum.de/doc/1379638/776321.pdf): 

```
@inproceedings{Althoff2017a,
	author = {Althoff, Matthias and Koschi, Markus and Manzinger, Stefanie},
	title = {CommonRoad: Composable benchmarks for motion planning on roads},
	booktitle = {Proc. of the IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium},
	year = {2017},
}
```

functions.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_dynamics_std.vehicle_dynamics_std
documentation:
vehicle_dynamics_std - single-track drift model vehicle dynamics

Syntax:

: f = vehicle_dynamics_std(x,u_init,p)

Inputs:
: ```
  param x

  vehicle state vector

  param u_init

  vehicle input vector

  param p

  vehicle parameter vector
  ```

Outputs:
: ```
  return f

  right-hand side of differential equations
  ```

Author: Gerald Würsching
Written: 23-October-2020
Last update: 23-October-2020

> 02-February-2021

Last revision: ---

functions.name: vehiclemodels.init_kst.init_kst
documentation:
init_KST - generates the initial state vector for the kinematic single-track model with on-axle trailer

Syntax:

: x0 = init_KST(init_state, p)

Inputs:
: ```
  param init_state

  core initial states

  param alpha0

  initial hitch angle
  ```

Outputs:
: ```
  return x0

  initial state vector
  ```

Author: Gerald Würsching
Written: 21-October-2020
Last update: 21-October-2020
Last revision: ---

functions.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_dynamics_linearized._interpolate_angle
documentation:
Interpolates an angle value between two angles according to the minimal value of the absolute difference
\:param x: value of other dimension to interpolate
\:param x1: lower bound of the other dimension
\:param x2: upper bound of the other dimension
\:param y1: lower bound of angle to interpolate
\:param y2: upper bound of angle to interpolate
\:return: interpolated angular value (in rad)

functions.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_dynamics_linearized.vehicle_dynamics_linearized
documentation:
linearized kinematic single-track model: separation of longitudinal and lateral motion
see: Pek, C. and Althoff, M. "Computationally Efficient Fail-safe Trajectory Planning for Self-driving Vehicles
Using Convex Optimization", ITSC, 2018

Inputs:
: ```
  param x

  combined (lon and lat) vehicle state vector [s, v, a, j, d, theta, kappa, kappa_dot]

  param u_init

  vehicle input vector [j_dot, kappa_dot_dot]

  param p

  vehicle parameters

  param ref_pos

  reference path position array (s coordinate)

  param ref_theta

  reference path orientation array (theta)
  ```

Outputs:
: ```
  return f

  right-hand side of differential equations
  ```

Class defines all trailer parameters (for on-axle trailer-truck models)

functions.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.setup_vehicle_parameters
documentation:
Creates an OmegaConf DictConfig object holding all vehicle parameters for a given vehicle type ID
The parameters are read from the YAML-files in vehiclemodels/parameters or from the folder specified by dir_params
\:param vehicle_id: CommonRoad vehicle ID (see Althoff, M. and Würsching, G. "CommonRoad: Vehicle Models", 2020)
\:param dir_params: [optional] path to folder containing the parameter yaml files. If None, the default location
in /vehiclemodels/parameters is used
\:return OmegaConf.DictConfig object with vehicle parameters

classes.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.a
documentation: distance from spring mass center of gravity to front axle [m]  LENA

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.b
documentation: distance from spring mass center of gravity to rear axle [m]  LENB

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_Phi_s
documentation: moment of inertia for sprung mass in roll [kg m^2]  IXS

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_y_s
documentation: moment of inertia for sprung mass in pitch [kg m^2]  IYS

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_z
documentation: moment of inertia for sprung mass in yaw [kg m^2]  IZZ

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_xz_s
documentation: moment of inertia cross product [kg m^2]  IXZ

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_sf
documentation: suspension spring rate (front) [N/m]  KSF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_sdf
documentation: suspension damping rate (front) [N s/m]  KSDF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_sr
documentation: suspension spring rate (rear) [N/m]  KSR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_sdr
documentation: suspension damping rate (rear) [N s/m]  KSDR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.T_f
documentation: track width front [m]  TRWF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.T_r
documentation: track width rear [m]  TRWB

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_ras
documentation: lateral spring rate at compliant compliant pin joint between M_s and M_u [N/m]  KRAS

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_tsf
documentation: auxiliary torsion roll stiffness per axle (normally negative) (front) [N m/rad]  KTSF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_tsr
documentation: auxiliary torsion roll stiffness per axle (normally negative) (rear) [N m/rad]  KTSR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_rad
documentation: damping rate at compliant compliant pin joint between M_s and M_u [N s/m]  KRADP

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_zt
documentation: vertical spring rate of tire [N/m]  TSPRINGR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.h_cg
documentation: center of gravity height of total mass [m]  HCG (mainly required for conversion to other vehicle models)

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.h_raf
documentation: height of roll axis above ground (front) [m]  HRAF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.h_rar
documentation: height of roll axis above ground (rear) [m]  HRAR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.h_s
documentation: M_s center of gravity above ground [m]  HS

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_uf
documentation: moment of inertia for unsprung mass about x-axis (front) [kg m^2]  IXUF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_ur
documentation: moment of inertia for unsprung mass about x-axis (rear) [kg m^2]  IXUR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.I_y_w
documentation: wheel inertia, from internet forum for 235/65 R 17 [kg m^2]

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.K_lt
documentation: lateral compliance rate of tire, wheel, and suspension, per tire [m/N]  KLT

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.R_w
documentation: effective wheel/tire radius  chosen as tire rolling radius RR  taken from ADAMS documentation [m]

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.D_f
documentation: [rad/m]  DF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.D_r
documentation: [rad/m]  DR

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.E_f
documentation: [needs conversion if nonzero]  EF

class_variables.name: vehiclemodels.vehicle_parameters.VehicleParameters.E_r
documentation: [needs conversion if nonzero]  ER

VehicleParameters base class: defines all parameters used by the vehicle models described in
Althoff, M. and Würsching, G. "CommonRoad: Vehicle Models", 2020

functions.name: vehiclemodels.utils.acceleration_constraints.acceleration_constraints
documentation:
accelerationConstraints - adjusts the acceleration based on acceleration constraints

Inputs:
    :param acceleration - acceleration in driving direction
    :param velocity - velocity in driving direction
    :params p - longitudinal parameter structure

Outputs:
    :return acceleration - acceleration in driving direction

Author: Matthias Althoff
Written: 15-December-2017
Last update: ---
Last revision: ---
